title: Biology
description: ZIMSEC Advanced Level Biology provides a comprehensive understanding of biological principles, fostering critical thinking and problem-solving skills. The syllabus encourages research into contemporary issues and promotes the sustainable utilization of local flora and fauna. It emphasizes practical application and inquiry-based learning, empowering students to make informed decisions about health and environmental conservation.

topics.title: Insulin Production
topics.description: This topic outlines the steps involved in the bacterial synthesis of human insulin and explains the therapeutic advantages for diabetics.

topics.title: Genetic Screening and Fingerprinting
topics.description: Explores the procedures and theoretical basis of genetic screening and DNA fingerprinting, including their roles in identifying genetic conditions and for forensic purposes.

topics.title: Gene Therapy
topics.description: This section introduces the fundamental principles and applications of gene therapy as a medical intervention.

topics.title: Benefits and Hazards of Gene Technology
topics.description: Discusses the various advantages and potential risks associated with the application of gene technology.

topics.title: Ethical implications of Gene Technology
topics.description: Examines the social and ethical considerations arising from advancements and applications in gene technology.

topics.title: Natural Selection
topics.description: Explains how mutations and environmental factors influence phenotype and drive the process of natural selection leading to evolution.

topics.title: Artificial Selection
topics.description: Describes examples and principles of artificial selection, highlighting human influence on the genetic makeup of populations.

topics.title: Mammalian Circulatory System
topics.description: Identifies the structures of arteries, veins, and capillaries, explains the role of haemoglobin and the Bohr effect in oxygen and carbon dioxide transport, and describes the cardiac cycle.

topics.title: Need for Communication
topics.description: Recognizes the fundamental requirement for efficient communication systems within living organisms.

topics.title: Action Potential
topics.description: Describes the generation and transmission of an action potential along myelinated neurons, emphasizing the roles of myelin sheath and refractory period in impulse speed.

topics.title: Sexual Reproduction in Plants
topics.description: Describes anther structure, pollen formation, ovule development, double fertilization, and its significance in plant reproduction.

topics.title: Sexual Reproduction in Humans
topics.description: Recognizes the microscopic structures of the ovary and testis, describes gametogenesis, hormonal control, fertilization, placenta structure and roles, contraception, and abortion.

topics.title: Levels of Ecological Organisation
topics.description: Defines terms such as species, habitat, population, niche, community, and ecosystem in the context of ecological organization.

topics.title: Nitrogen Cycle
topics.description: Outlines the nitrogen cycle, detailing the roles of nitrogen-fixing, nitrifying, and denitrifying bacteria.

topics.title: Anthropogenic Impact on Ecosystems
topics.description: Describes the effects of human activities like human settlement, deforestation, industrial activities, agriculture, mining, global warming, and invasive plant species on ecosystems.

topics.title: Conservation
topics.description: Explains conservation methods including preservation, management, and reclamation, with specific examples like African Elephants and White Rhinoceros, also discussing the role of EMA and CAMPFIRE.

topics.title: Classification
topics.description: Identifies organisms using diagnostic features of the five Kingdoms and phyla, states the taxonomic hierarchy, and observes the rules of binomial nomenclature.

topics.title: Importance of Biodiversity
topics.description: Describes the socio-economic importance of the five Kingdoms (Prokaryotae, Protista, Fungi, Plantae, Animalia) in various aspects like fermentation, biotechnology, food, decomposition, medicine, and tourism.
